sentences.sentence: Of all mankind's manifold creations, language must take pride of place.
sentences.translation: 在人类种种创造之中，语言当属最重要的一项。

structure.text: Of all mankind's manifold creations
language
must take pride of place

difficult_points: Of all...creations 是介词短语作状语，表示比较的范围。
take pride of place 是固定表达，意为“占据首位、最为重要”。

sentences.sentence: Other inventions - the wheel, agriculture, sliced bread - may have transformed our material existence, but the advent of language is what made us human.
sentences.translation: 其他发明——车轮、农业、切片面包——或许改变了我们的物质生活，但语言的出现才真正使我们成为人类。

structure.text: Other inventions
the wheel, agriculture, sliced bread
may have transformed our material existence
but the advent of language is what made us human
the advent of language
is
what made us human

difficult_points: 破折号中的三个名词短语是 other inventions 的同位语，具体列举实例。
what made us human 是 what 引导的名词性从句，作系动词 is 的表语。
may have transformed 是情态动词加现在完成式，表示对过去的推测。

sentences.sentence: Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance, since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it.
sentences.translation: 与语言相比，其他一切发明都黯然失色，因为我们所取得的一切成就都依赖于语言、并源自语言。

structure.text: Compared to language
all other inventions
pale in significance
since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it
everything we have ever achieved
we have ever achieved
depends on language and originates from it

difficult_points: Compared to language 是过去分词短语作状语，相当于 when compared to language。
everything 后省略了关系代词 that/which，we have ever achieved 是省略引导词的定语从句。
depends on... 和 originates from... 是两个并列谓语，共用主语 everything...achieved。

sentences.sentence: Without language, we could never have embarked on our ascent to unparalleled power over all other animals, and even over nature itself.
sentences.translation: 没有语言，我们就永远无法踏上凌驾于其他一切动物、乃至自然本身之上、获得无与伦比力量的历程。

structure.text: Without language
we
could never have embarked on our ascent to unparalleled power over all other animals
and even over nature itself

difficult_points: Without language 相当于与过去事实相反的虚拟条件，等于 If we hadn't had language。
could never have embarked 是情态动词加完成式，表示对过去情况的假设推测。
and even over nature itself 与前面 over all other animals 并列，itself 强调“自然本身”。

sentences.sentence: But language is foremost not just because it came first.
sentences.translation: 但语言之所以首屈一指，并不仅仅因为它最先出现。

structure.text: language
is foremost
not just because it came first

difficult_points: not just because... 否定的是原因状语，而非整个主句，意为“不仅仅因为……”。
foremost 在此作表语，意为“最重要的；首要的”。

sentences.sentence: In its own right it is a tool of extraordinary sophistication, yet based on an idea of ingenious simplicity: 'this marvellous invention of composing out of twenty-five or thirty sounds that infinite variety of expressions which, whilst having in themselves no likeness to what is in our mind, allow us to disclose to others its whole secret, and to make known to those who cannot penetrate it all that we imagine, and all the various stirrings of our soul'.
sentences.translation: 语言本身就是一件极为精巧复杂的工具，却又建立在一个绝妙的简单构想之上：“这项奇妙的发明，用二十五六个乃至三十来个声音，组合出无穷多样的表达方式；这些表达方式本身与我们心中所想毫无相似之处，却能让我们向他人揭示内心的全部秘密，让那些无法看透我们内心的人，也能知晓我们所想象的一切、以及内心深处种种情感的涌动。”

structure.text: In its own right
it
is a tool of extraordinary sophistication
yet based on an idea of ingenious simplicity
this marvellous invention of composing out of twenty-five or thirty sounds that infinite variety of expressions

which, whilst having in themselves no likeness to what is in our mind, allow us to disclose to others its whole secret, and to make known to those who cannot penetrate it all that we imagine, and all the various stirrings of our soul
whilst having in themselves no likeness to what is in our mind
what is in our mind
allow us to disclose to others its whole secret
to disclose to others its whole secret
and to make known to those who cannot penetrate it all that we imagine, and all the various stirrings of our soul
to make known to those who cannot penetrate it all that we imagine, and all the various stirrings of our soul
who cannot penetrate it
all that we imagine

difficult_points: yet based on... 是过去分词短语，省略了 it is，与主句并列说明语言这一工具的另一特点。
冒号后的引文是对 an idea of ingenious simplicity 的具体解释说明。
which 引导的关系从句修饰 that infinite variety of expressions，从句中 whilst having... 是让步性伴随状语。
and to make known... 与 to disclose... 并列，均为 allow us to 结构中的不定式补足语。

sentences.sentence: This was how, in 1660, the renowned French grammarians of the Port-Royal abbey near Versailles distilled the essence of language, and no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement.
sentences.translation: 1660年，凡尔赛附近波尔罗雅尔修道院那些声名卓著的法国语法学家们，正是这样提炼出了语言的本质；此后再没有人如此雄辩地赞颂过这一成就的伟大。

structure.text: This
was
how, in 1660, the renowned French grammarians of the Port-Royal abbey near Versailles distilled the essence of language
the renowned French grammarians of the Port-Royal abbey near Versailles
distilled the essence of language
and no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement

difficult_points: This was how... 是 how 引导的表语从句，说明前述引文正是语法学家们提炼语言本质的方式。
since 在此作副词，意为“此后”，修饰 has celebrated。
no one...has celebrated more eloquently 使用完成时态，强调迄今为止无人能超越。

sentences.sentence: Even so, there is just one flaw in all these hymns of praise, for the homage to language's unique accomplishment conceals a simple yet critical incongruity.
sentences.translation: 即便如此，这些赞美之词中仍存在一处瑕疵：对语言独特成就的推崇，掩盖了一个简单却关键的矛盾之处。

structure.text: Even so
there is just one flaw in all these hymns of praise
for the homage to language's unique accomplishment conceals a simple yet critical incongruity
the homage to language's unique accomplishment
conceals a simple yet critical incongruity

difficult_points: Even so 是连接副词，表示转折，意为“即便如此”。
for 在此是并列连词，表示原因，相当于 because。
homage to... 中的 to 是介词，homage 的逻辑对象是 language's unique accomplishment。

expressions.meaning_zh: 关键性的矛盾、不协调之处

sentences.sentence: Language is mankind's greatest invention – except, of course, that it was never invented.
sentences.translation: 语言是人类最伟大的发明——当然，只不过它从未被真正发明出来。

structure.text: Language
is mankind's greatest invention
except, of course, that it was never invented
that it was never invented

difficult_points: except that... 表示例外，意为“只不过……”，对前面的说法作出修正。
of course 是插入语，加强语气，意为“当然”。
was never invented 使用被动语态，呼应下文语言并非被人为发明的悖论。

sentences.sentence: This apparent paradox is at the core of our fascination with language, and it holds many of its secrets.
sentences.translation: 这一看似矛盾之处，正是我们痴迷于语言的核心所在，其中也蕴藏着语言的诸多秘密。

structure.text: This apparent paradox
is at the core of our fascination with language
and it holds many of its secrets

difficult_points: apparent 意为“表面上的、看似的”，暗示这一悖论并非真正矛盾。
fascination with 表示“对……的痴迷”，with 引出痴迷的对象。
it 指代前面提到的 this apparent paradox。

sentences.sentence: Language often seems so skillfully drafted that one can hardly imagine it as anything other than the perfected handiwork of a master craftsman.
sentences.translation: 语言常常显得如此精巧，以至于让人几乎无法把它想象成别的什么，而只能认为它是一位大师级工匠的完美杰作。

structure.text: Language
often seems so skillfully drafted
that one can hardly imagine it as anything other than the perfected handiwork of a master craftsman
one
can hardly imagine it as anything other than the perfected handiwork of a master craftsman

difficult_points: so...that... 是结果状语从句结构，表示“如此……以至于……”。
anything other than 意为“除……之外的任何东西”，与 hardly 呼应，表达强烈的排除语气。
drafted 本义为“起草”，此处比喻语言像被精心设计出来一样。

sentences.sentence: How else could this instrument make so much out of barely three dozen measly morsels of sound?
sentences.translation: 否则，这一工具究竟怎么可能仅凭区区三十来个微不足道的音节碎片，就创造出如此丰富的内容呢？

structure.text: How else
could this instrument make so much
out of barely three dozen measly morsels of sound

difficult_points: How else 引导修辞疑问句，表示“除此之外还能怎样”，实为强调别无他法。
could this instrument make 是疑问句中的主谓倒装结构。
measly morsels of sound 是形象化表达，形容声音碎片“少得可怜”。

sentences.sentence: In themselves, these configurations of mouth – p, f, b, v, t, d, k, g, sh, a, e and so on – amount to nothing more than a few haphazard spits and splutters, random noises with no meaning, no ability to express, no power to explain.
sentences.translation: 这些口腔发出的构型——p、f、b、v、t、d、k、g、sh、a、e等等——本身不过是一些杂乱无章的吐音和噗噗声，是毫无意义、毫无表达能力、毫无解释能力的随机噪音而已。

structure.text: In themselves
these configurations of mouth
p, f, b, v, t, d, k, g, sh, a, e and so on
amount to nothing more than a few haphazard spits and splutters, random noises with no meaning, no ability to express, no power to explain

difficult_points: In themselves 意为“就其本身而言”，强调孤立地看待这些声音。
破折号中的字母列举是 these configurations of mouth 的同位语，具体说明。
amount to nothing more than 意为“不过是；仅仅等于”。
no meaning, no ability to express, no power to explain 三个并列的 no+名词短语，共同修饰 random noises。

sentences.sentence: But run them through the cogs and wheels of the language machine, let it arrange them in some very special orders, and there is nothing that these meaningless streams of air cannot do: from sighing the interminable boredom of existence to unravelling the fundamental order of the universe.
sentences.translation: 但是，一旦把这些声音送入语言机器的齿轮和轮盘，让它以某些特殊的顺序加以排列，这些毫无意义的气流便无所不能——从倾诉存在的无尽乏味，到揭示宇宙的根本秩序。

structure.text: run them through the cogs and wheels of the language machine
let it arrange them in some very special orders
and there is nothing
that these meaningless streams of air cannot do
from sighing the interminable boredom of existence to unravelling the fundamental order of the universe

difficult_points: run them...和 let it arrange... 是两个祈使句并列，实际相当于条件状语，意为“只要……”。
there is nothing that...cannot do 是双重否定结构，表示“无所不能”。
from...to... 列举两个动名词短语，说明语言从平凡琐事到深奥道理无所不能表达。

expressions.meaning_zh: 齿轮和轮子；(喻)运作机制

sentences.sentence: The most extraordinary thing about language, however, is that one doesn't have to be a genius to set its wheels in motion.
sentences.translation: 然而，关于语言最不同寻常的一点在于，人们无需是天才，就能让它的机器运转起来。

structure.text: The most extraordinary thing about language
however
is
that one doesn't have to be a genius to set its wheels in motion
to set its wheels in motion

difficult_points: however 是插入的连接副词，起转折作用，不影响句子主干结构。
that... 引导表语从句，说明 the most extraordinary thing 具体所指。
doesn't have to 表示“不必”，与“没有能力”含义不同，不要混淆。
set its wheels in motion 是比喻表达，把语言比作一台机器。

sentences.sentence: The language machine allows just about everybody - from pre-modern foragers in the subtropical savannah, to post-modern philosophers in the suburban sprawl - to tie these meaningless sounds together into an infinite variety of subtle senses, and all apparently without the slightest exertion.
sentences.translation: 语言机器几乎让每一个人——从亚热带草原上的前现代采集者，到城郊蔓延地带的后现代哲学家——都能把这些毫无意义的声音编织成无穷多样、微妙精细的意义，而且看起来毫不费力。

structure.text: The language machine
allows just about everybody
from pre-modern foragers in the subtropical savannah, to post-modern philosophers in the suburban sprawl
to tie these meaningless sounds together into an infinite variety of subtle senses
and all apparently without the slightest exertion

difficult_points: 破折号中的 from...to... 具体说明 everybody 所涵盖的范围极广。
allow sb. to do 结构中，不定式 to tie... 是 allows 的宾语补足语。
and all apparently without... 省略了谓语，承接前文补充说明“毫不费力”。

sentences.sentence: Yet it is precisely this deceptive ease which makes language a victim of its own success, since in everyday life its triumphs are usually taken for granted.
sentences.translation: 然而，恰恰是这种带有欺骗性的轻松，使语言成了自身成功的牺牲品，因为在日常生活中，人们往往把语言的胜利视为理所当然。

structure.text: Yet
it is precisely this deceptive ease which makes language a victim of its own success
this deceptive ease
which makes language a victim of its own success
since in everyday life its triumphs are usually taken for granted
its triumphs
are usually taken for granted

difficult_points: it is...which... 是强调句结构，突出 this deceptive ease。
a victim of its own success 意为“因自身的成功而受累”，喻指因太容易而被人忽视。
take...for granted 意为“想当然、不予重视”，此处使用被动语态。

expressions.text: a victim of its own success
expressions.meaning_zh: 因自身成功而受累（成也自己，败也自己）

sentences.sentence: The wheels of language run so smoothly that one rarely bothers to stop and think about all the resourcefulness and expertise that must have gone into making it tick.
sentences.translation: 语言这台机器运转得如此顺畅，以至于很少有人会停下来想一想，让它运转起来到底需要投入多少智慧和专业技能。

structure.text: The wheels of language
run so smoothly
that one rarely bothers to stop and think about all the resourcefulness and expertise
that must have gone into making it tick
making it tick

difficult_points: so...that... 结构表示程度和结果，意为“如此……以至于……”。
bother to do 表示“费心去做某事”，常用于否定或半否定语境。
that must have gone into... 是修饰 resourcefulness and expertise 的定语从句，must have gone into 表示对过去情况的推测。
make it tick 是习语，意为“使……运转起来”。

sentences.sentence: Language conceals art.
sentences.translation: 语言把技艺隐藏了起来。

structure.text: Language
conceals art

difficult_points: 全句仅三个词，是对本段核心观点的高度凝练概括。
art 在此指“技艺、匠心”，呼应上文提到的 resourcefulness and expertise。

sentences.sentence: Often, it is only the estrangement of foreign tongues, with their many exotic and outlandish features, that brings home the wonder of language's design.
sentences.translation: 通常，只有当我们接触到那些具有诸多奇异特征的外语、感受到其中的陌生感时，才会真正体会到语言设计的奇妙之处。

structure.text: Often
it is only the estrangement of foreign tongues
with their many exotic and outlandish features
that brings home the wonder of language's design

difficult_points: it is...that... 是强调句型，突出 the estrangement of foreign tongues。
with their many exotic...features 是伴随状语，补充说明外语的特点。
bring home 是短语动词，意为“使人深刻认识到”。

sentences.sentence: One of the showiest stunts that some languages can pull off is an ability to build up words of breath-breaking length, and thus express in one word what English takes a whole sentence to say.
sentences.translation: 有些语言能够玩出的最惊艳的花活之一，就是构造出令人叹为观止的超长单词，从而用一个词表达出英语需要一整句话才能表达的意思。

structure.text: One of the showiest stunts
that some languages can pull off
is an ability to build up words of breath-breaking length
to build up words of breath-breaking length
and thus express in one word what English takes a whole sentence to say
what English takes a whole sentence to say

difficult_points: that some languages can pull off 是定语从句，修饰 stunts。
pull off 意为“成功做成（难事）”。
what English takes a whole sentence to say 是宾语从句，作 express 的宾语，what 指代所表达的内容。

sentences.sentence: The Turkish word şehriliştiremediklerimizdensiniz, to take one example, means nothing less than 'you are one of those whom we can't turn into a town-dweller'.
sentences.translation: 举个例子，土耳其语单词şehriliştiremediklerimizdensiniz的意思，正是“你是我们无法使其变成城里人的那些人当中的一个”。

structure.text: The Turkish word şehriliştiremediklerimizdensiniz
to take one example
means nothing less than
'you are one of those whom we can't turn into a town-dweller'
whom we can't turn into a town-dweller

difficult_points: to take one example 是插入的不定式短语，相当于 for example。
means nothing less than 意为“正是；不折不扣就是”，加强语气。
whom we can't turn into... 是定语从句，修饰 those，whom 作 turn 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: (In case you were wondering, this monstrosity really is one word, not merely many different words squashed together - most of its components cannot even stand up on their own.)
sentences.translation: （以防你产生疑问，这个庞然大物般的词确实是一个单词，而不只是许多不同的词硬凑在一起——它的大部分组成部分甚至根本无法独立成词。）

structure.text: In case you were wondering
this monstrosity really is one word
not merely many different words squashed together
squashed together
most of its components cannot even stand up on their own

difficult_points: In case you were wondering 是插入的条件状语从句，表示“以防你有疑问”。
not merely... 与前面 one word 形成对比，进一步排除误解。
squashed together 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 words。
stand up on their own 意为“独立存在”，此处指词的组成部分不能单独成词。

sentences.sentence: And if that sounds like some one-off freak, then consider Sumerian, the language spoken on the banks of the Euphrates some 5,000 years ago by the people who invented writing and thus enabled the documentation of history.
sentences.translation: 如果这听起来像是某种绝无仅有的特例，那么不妨想想苏美尔语——大约五千年前，由发明了文字、从而使历史得以被记录下来的那群人，在幼发拉底河畔所使用的语言。

structure.text: if that sounds like some one-off freak
then consider Sumerian
the language spoken on the banks of the Euphrates some 5,000 years ago by the people
spoken on the banks of the Euphrates some 5,000 years ago by the people
who invented writing and thus enabled the documentation of history

difficult_points: if that sounds like... 是条件状语从句，that 指代前文提到的土耳其语超长词现象。
the language spoken... 是 Sumerian 的同位语，spoken 后的过去分词短语说明该语言的使用情况。
by the people 引出动作发出者，who 引导的定语从句修饰 the people。

expressions.meaning_zh: 绝无仅有的；仅此一次的

sentences.sentence: A Sumerian word like munintuma'a ('when he had made it suitable for her') might seem rather trim compared to the Turkish colossus above.
sentences.translation: 像munintuma'a（“当他使它适合她之后”）这样的苏美尔语单词，与上文提到的那个土耳其语庞然大物相比，可能显得相当精简。

structure.text: A Sumerian word like munintuma'a
'when he had made it suitable for her'
might seem rather trim
compared to the Turkish colossus above

difficult_points: 括号内容是对 munintuma'a 词义的直接解释，作同位语。
compared to... 是过去分词短语作状语，相当于 when compared to。
might seem 表示委婉的推测语气。

sentences.sentence: What is so impressive about it, however, is not its lengthiness but rather the reverse - the thrifty compactness of its construction.
sentences.translation: 然而，这个词真正令人印象深刻之处，不在于它的长度，恰恰相反，而在于其构造上精打细算的紧凑性。

structure.text: What is so impressive about it
however
is not its lengthiness but rather the reverse
the thrifty compactness of its construction

difficult_points: What 引导的从句作整句主语，意为“真正令人印象深刻的东西”。
not A but rather B 表示“不是A而是B”。
破折号后的内容是对 the reverse 的具体说明。

expressions.meaning_zh: 令人印象深刻之处在于

sentences.sentence: The word is made up of different slots, each corresponding to a particular portion of meaning.
sentences.translation: 这个词由不同的“槽位”构成，每个槽位对应着意义的某一特定部分。

structure.text: The word
is made up of different slots
each corresponding to a particular portion of meaning

difficult_points: be made up of 意为“由……构成”。
each corresponding to... 是独立主格结构，each 指代 slots，说明每个槽位的功能。

sentences.sentence: This sleek design allows single sounds to convey useful information, and in fact even the absence of a sound has been enlisted to express something specific.
sentences.translation: 这种精巧的设计使得单个的声音就能传达出有用的信息，事实上，甚至声音的缺失也被用来表达某种特定的含义。

structure.text: This sleek design
allows single sounds to convey useful information
to convey useful information
and in fact even the absence of a sound has been enlisted to express something specific
to express something specific

difficult_points: allow sth. to do 结构中，to convey...是 single sounds 的宾语补足语。
has been enlisted 是被动语态，字面意为“被征用”，此处喻指“被用来”。
even the absence of a sound 强调连“无声”这一现象都被赋予了功能。

sentences.sentence: If you were to ask which bit in the Sumerian word corresponds to the pronoun 'it' in the English translation 'when he had made it suitable for her', then the answer would have to be nothing.
sentences.translation: 如果你要问，苏美尔语这个词里哪一部分对应英语译文“当他使它适合她之后”里的代词“it”，那么答案只能是：什么都没有。

structure.text: If you were to ask which bit in the Sumerian word corresponds to the pronoun 'it' in the English translation 'when he had made it suitable for her'
you
were to ask
which bit in the Sumerian word corresponds to the pronoun 'it' in the English translation 'when he had made it suitable for her'
then the answer would have to be nothing

difficult_points: were to ask 是 were to do 结构，表示对将来假设情况的虚拟语气。
which bit...引导的从句是 ask 的宾语从句。
would have to be 表示在假设条件下必然得出的结论。

sentences.sentence: Mind you, a very particular kind of nothing: the nothing that stands in the empty slot in the middle.
sentences.translation: 不过要注意，这可是一种非常特别的“无”——那种存在于中间那个空槽位里的“无”。

structure.text: Mind you
a very particular kind of nothing
the nothing that stands in the empty slot in the middle
that stands in the empty slot in the middle

difficult_points: Mind you 是插入语，用于提醒对方注意，意为“不过要注意”。
本句是不完整的名词短语，承接上一句的 nothing 作进一步说明，属修辞性的强调结构。
that stands in...是定语从句，修饰 the nothing。

sentences.sentence: The technology is so fine-tuned then that even a non-sound, when carefully placed in a particular position, has been invested with a specific function.
sentences.translation: 由此可见，这项“技术”被打磨得如此精细，以至于哪怕是一个“非声音”，只要被小心安放在特定位置，也被赋予了具体的功能。

structure.text: The technology
is so fine-tuned
then
that even a non-sound, when carefully placed in a particular position, has been invested with a specific function
even a non-sound
when carefully placed in a particular position
has been invested with a specific function

difficult_points: so...that... 结构表示程度与结果。
when carefully placed in a particular position 省略了主语和 be 动词，完整形式为 when it is carefully placed。
be invested with 意为“被赋予”，被动语态强调声音的位置本身被赋予了功能。

sentences.sentence: Who could possibly have come up with such a nifty contraption?
sentences.translation: 究竟是谁能想得出这么精巧的装置呢？

structure.text: Who
could possibly have come up with such a nifty contraption

difficult_points: could possibly have come up with 是情态动词加完成式，表示对过去可能性的感叹或反问。
全句是修辞疑问句，意在表达对苏美尔语构造之精巧的惊叹，而非真正提问。
